input.old_string:
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/sap-smoke-test.sh <sap-hostname> [dns-server]
input.new_string:
# NOTE: This script uses `date +%s%N` for nanosecond timing, which is
# Linux-specific (GNU coreutils). macOS `date` does not support %N.
# On macOS, install GNU coreutils (`brew install coreutils`) and use `gdate`,
# or run the script from an AKS pod / Linux VM as intended.
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/sap-smoke-test.sh <sap-hostname> [dns-server]

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Node Failure Resilience Test (Story 1.2, AC4 / NFR11)
#
# Verifies that a single node failure does not disrupt workload availability.
# Simulates failure by cordoning and draining a user (workload) node, then
# confirms that pods reschedule to remaining nodes within acceptable time.
#
# This script is NON-DESTRUCTIVE to data but WILL evict pods from the target node.
# Use during maintenance windows or in DEV environments.
#
# Usage:
#   ./node-failure-test.sh [--auto-uncordon]
#
# Options:
#   --auto-uncordon  Automatically restore the node after test (default: manual)
#
# Prerequisites:
#   - kubectl configured with AKS cluster credentials
#   - Cluster admin or sufficient RBAC permissions for drain/cordon
#
# Expected outcome (NFR11):
#   - All workload pods reschedule to healthy nodes
#   - No CronJob executions are missed during the drain
#   - Cluster returns to full capacity after uncordon

set -euo pipefail

AUTO_UNCORDON=false
if [[ "${1:-}" == "--auto-uncordon" ]]; then
  AUTO_UNCORDON=true
fi

PASS=0
FAIL=0

check_pass() { echo "  PASS: $1"; ((PASS++)); }
check_fail() { echo "  FAIL: $1"; ((FAIL++)); }

echo "=== Node Failure Resilience Test (AC4 / NFR11) ==="
echo ""

# Step 1: Identify workload node pool nodes
echo "--- Step 1: Identify workload pool nodes ---"
WORKLOAD_NODES=$(kubectl get nodes -l agentpool=workload --no-headers -o custom-columns=":metadata.name" 2>/dev/null)

if [ -z "${WORKLOAD_NODES}" ]; then
  echo "  No workload pool nodes found (label: agentpool=workload)"
  echo "  Trying alternative: nodes without system taint..."
  WORKLOAD_NODES=$(kubectl get nodes --no-headers -o custom-columns=":metadata.name" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "system" | head -5)
fi

NODE_COUNT=$(echo "${WORKLOAD_NODES}" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
echo "  Found ${NODE_COUNT} workload node(s)"

if [ "${NODE_COUNT}" -lt 2 ]; then
  check_fail "Need at least 2 workload nodes for resilience test (found ${NODE_COUNT})"
  echo "ABORT: Cannot test node failure resilience with fewer than 2 nodes"
  exit 1
fi
check_pass "Sufficient workload nodes for resilience test (${NODE_COUNT})"

# Select the first workload node as drain target
TARGET_NODE=$(echo "${WORKLOAD_NODES}" | head -1)
echo "  Target node for drain: ${TARGET_NODE}"
echo ""

# Step 2: Record pre-drain state
echo "--- Step 2: Record pre-drain state ---"
PRE_PODS=$(kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector "spec.nodeName=${TARGET_NODE}" --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -v 'kube-system' | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
PRE_READY=$(kubectl get nodes --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c ' Ready ' || echo 0)
echo "  Pods on target node (non-system): ${PRE_PODS}"
echo "  Ready nodes before drain: ${PRE_READY}"
echo ""

# Step 3: Cordon and drain the target node
echo "--- Step 3: Cordon and drain target node ---"
echo "  Cordoning ${TARGET_NODE}..."
kubectl cordon "${TARGET_NODE}"
check_pass "Node cordoned: ${TARGET_NODE}"

echo "  Draining ${TARGET_NODE} (timeout: 120s)..."
if kubectl drain "${TARGET_NODE}" \
  --ignore-daemonsets \
  --delete-emptydir-data \
  --timeout=120s \
  --grace-period=30 2>&1; then
  check_pass "Node drained successfully"
else
  check_fail "Node drain failed or timed out"
fi
echo ""

# Step 4: Verify pod rescheduling
echo "--- Step 4: Verify pod rescheduling ---"
echo "  Waiting 30 seconds for pods to reschedule..."
sleep 30

POST_READY=$(kubectl get nodes --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c ' Ready ' || echo 0)
REMAINING_READY=$((POST_READY))
echo "  Ready nodes after drain: ${REMAINING_READY} (was ${PRE_READY})"

if [ "${REMAINING_READY}" -ge $((PRE_READY - 1)) ]; then
  check_pass "Cluster maintains availability with one node drained"
else
  check_fail "Unexpected node count drop (${REMAINING_READY} ready, expected $((PRE_READY - 1))+)"
fi

# Check that no pods are stuck in Pending
PENDING_PODS=$(kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'Pending' || echo 0)
if [ "${PENDING_PODS}" -eq 0 ]; then
  check_pass "No pods stuck in Pending state after drain"
else
  check_fail "${PENDING_PODS} pod(s) stuck in Pending after drain"
fi
echo ""

# Step 5: Restore node (if auto-uncordon)
echo "--- Step 5: Node restoration ---"
if [ "${AUTO_UNCORDON}" = true ]; then
  echo "  Uncordoning ${TARGET_NODE}..."
  kubectl uncordon "${TARGET_NODE}"
  check_pass "Node restored: ${TARGET_NODE}"
  echo "  Waiting 15 seconds for node to rejoin..."
  sleep 15
  FINAL_READY=$(kubectl get nodes --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c ' Ready ' || echo 0)
  echo "  Ready nodes after uncordon: ${FINAL_READY}"
else
  echo "  Node ${TARGET_NODE} is still cordoned."
  echo "  Run: kubectl uncordon ${TARGET_NODE}"
  echo "  to restore the node to the pool."
fi
echo ""

# Summary
echo "==========================================="
echo "  Resilience Test Summary"
echo "==========================================="
echo "  PASS: ${PASS}"
echo "  FAIL: ${FAIL}"
echo "  Target node: ${TARGET_NODE}"
echo ""

if [ "${FAIL}" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "  RESULT: FAIL — cluster did not maintain availability"
  exit 1
else
  echo "  RESULT: PASS — single node failure handled correctly (NFR11)"
  exit 0
fi
